title: cyperus antillanus
intro: Cyperus antillanus, also known as Antillean flatsedge, is a plant species belonging to the sedge family, characterized by its distinctive flat or rounded stems and small, yellowish or reddish-brown flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Cyperus antillanus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional medicine as a topical application for skin conditions and wounds.
